name: penumbra-glsl-01-cpig-gyroid
description: CPig live preview through the IN-PROCESS GLSL studio (Swath C). Loads 53_penumbra_preview.json via the Slop loader (Field TPMS gyroid ∩ sphere → CPig Penumbra Preview), sets the preview backend to GLSL (PenumbraPreviewBackend Glsl), then Build → CPig pushes its .3mf which routes to a device-free node compile and renders in the in-process GLSL conduit (native depth, camera-tracked) — NOT the out-of-process image-blit overlay. Gates the capture on WaitForPenumbraFrame (now wired for the GLSL conduit too) + captures the VIEWPORT (CaptureToBitmap includes the conduit's PostDrawObjects GL, unlike the OOP DrawForeground blit). Requires: Penumbra.Rhino plug-in auto-loaded + the CPig .gha (Penumbra Preview, GUID …BA200) loaded; the GLSL backend spawns a device-free node compile host on first push. MVP renders STRUCTURAL (tape) fields — a dense field would log a gl.compile3mf error (use the deprecated `penumbra` OOP suite for those until dense-field packing lands). mode: capture (no baseline; inspect the candidate + the gl.scene.loaded / gl.compile3mf telemetry).

setup.vlmDescription: A Penumbra SDF render of a gyroid lattice (smooth, repeating, interconnected curved surfaces) filling a roughly spherical region, ray-marched directly into the Rhino viewport (sharp, shaded, depth-correct). NOT a plain featureless sphere, NOT an empty grey viewport, NOT a flat 2D overlay.
